name: Unsafe deserialization via BinaryFormatter
description: BinaryFormatter.Deserialize, SoapFormatter.Deserialize, NetDataContractSerializer.Deserialize, or JsonConvert.DeserializeObject with TypeNameHandling is invoked on untrusted data. These APIs instantiate arbitrary types and invoke their constructors, enabling reliable remote code execution against any process that deserializes attacker-controlled input.
remediation: Do not use BinaryFormatter, SoapFormatter, or NetDataContractSerializer on untrusted data — these APIs are deprecated as of .NET 5 for this exact reason. Use System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer with strongly-typed target classes. If you must use Newtonsoft.Json, set TypeNameHandling = TypeNameHandling.None (the default).

patterns.message_template: BinaryFormatter.Deserialize is unsafe on untrusted data
SoapFormatter.Deserialize is unsafe on untrusted data
NetDataContractSerializer.Deserialize is unsafe on untrusted data

examples.vulnerable:
var formatter = new BinaryFormatter();
var obj = formatter.Deserialize(Request.Body);
